title: For Loops
overview: Loops let you repeat code multiple times without writing it over and over. The for loop is perfect for working with sequences of items.

blocks.title: What is a Loop?
blocks.body: A **loop** repeats a block of code multiple times. Instead of writing the same code 10 times, you write it once inside a loop.
bullets: You control how many times the loop runs
Each repetition is called an iteration

blocks.title: A Loop is Like a Washing Machine Cycle
blocks.text: You load clothes, set the timer, and the machine repeats the cycle until time runs out. A for loop processes each item in a list automatically.

blocks.title: Think Of A For Loop As A Washing Machine Cycle

leftPanel.label: Real Life Analogy
leftPanel.title: Washing Machine Cycle
leftPanel.description: The machine runs through each step automatically for every item

centerPanel.label: Programming Concept
centerPanel.textContent:
For each item in the list:
1. Take the next item
2. Process it
3. Move to the next
4. Repeat until done

rightPanel.label: Code Example
rightPanel.code:
for i in range(3):
    print(f'Wash cycle {i + 1}')
rightPanel.output:
Wash cycle 1
Wash cycle 2
Wash cycle 3

blocks.question: If you use for i in range(3), does the loop body run 3 times or 4 times?
blocks.answer: The loop body runs exactly 3 times — for i = 0, 1, and 2. range(3) gives you the numbers 0, 1, 2 (three numbers total). The loop stops after processing the last number, just like a washing machine stops after its final spin cycle.

blocks.title: Basic for Loop with a List
blocks.expectedOutput:
I like apple
I like banana
I like cherry

blocks.title: Iterate a List
blocks.instructions: Create a list of three colors. Use a for loop to print each color.
blocks.hint: Use for color in colors: and indent the print() statement.
blocks.expectedOutput:
Color: red
Color: blue
Color: green

blocks.title: Counting with range()
blocks.body: The `range()` function generates a sequence of numbers. Perfect when you want to repeat a specific number of times.
bullets: range(start, stop) — numbers from start to stop-1
range(start, stop, step) — numbers with custom step

blocks.title: range() Basics
range() with Step
blocks.code:
print("Even numbers:")
for i in range(0, 10, 2):
    print(i)

print("Countdown:")
for i in range(5, 0, -1):
    print(i)
print("Blast off!")

blocks.title: Print 1 to 10
blocks.instructions: Use a for loop with range() to print numbers 1 through 10.
blocks.hint: Use range(1, 11) — range stops before the end value.

blocks.title: Loops Inside Loops
blocks.body: You can put a loop inside another loop. The inner loop runs completely for EACH iteration of the outer loop.
bullets: Nested loops are great for patterns and tables

blocks.title: Multiplication Table
Multiplication Table
blocks.instructions: Ask for a number. Print its multiplication table from 1 to 10.
blocks.hint: Loop from 1 to 10. Inside the loop, print num * i.

keyTakeaways.text: Loops repeat code without writing it multiple times
For loops iterate over each item in a sequence
Nested loops are useful for tables and patterns

whatYouLearned: How for loops iterate over sequences
How to use range() for counting loops
